H.name: H
H.safe_description: One of the most common maternal lineages in Europe and the Near East. It may reflect deep origins reaching back tens of thousands of years before present.

U.name: U
U.safe_description: An ancient maternal lineage widespread across Eurasia, often found among the earliest hunter-gatherer populations of Europe.

L.name: L
L.safe_description: The deepest rooted branches of the human maternal tree, found predominantly in Africa and representing some of the earliest continuous human populations.

A.name: A
A.safe_description: Common in East Asia and the Americas, often associated with populations that expanded from Asia across the Bering land bridge.

unknown.name: Unassigned
unknown.safe_description: Maternal lineage not identified in the provided data, or falls into a rare sub-clade.

R.name: R
R.safe_description: A very common paternal lineage in Europe, Central Asia, and South Asia, often associated with historical migrations of pastoralist societies.

E.name: E
E.safe_description: Widespread across Africa, the Middle East, and parts of Europe. It is commonly associated with significant population expansions on the African continent.

O.name: O
O.safe_description: The predominant paternal lineage in East and Southeast Asia, reflecting vast population developments in the region.

I.name: I
I.safe_description: A paternal lineage almost entirely restricted to Europe, generally tracing back to early hunter-gatherer populations of the continent.

unknown.name: Unassigned
unknown.safe_description: Paternal lineage not identified in the provided data.
